---
title: "Resume"
description: "Education, awards, teaching, and a downloadable resume."
---

::: {.two-col}
::: {}
I am a computational physicist and scientific machine learning researcher specializing in high-performance multi-physics simulation, energetic materials, shock physics, and physics-aware deep learning.

My work spans solver development, microstructure-resolved DNS, HPC production campaigns, and simulation-grounded ML evaluation for energetic-material response under extreme loading.

<div class="actions">
  <a class="button primary" href="assets/Shobhan_Roy_Industry_Resume.pdf">Download Resume</a>
  <a class="button" href="publications.qmd">Publications</a>
</div>
:::

::: {.callout}
### Current Focus

- Shock-to-detonation simulation
- Energetic-material microstructures
- Physics-aware surrogate modeling
- HPC-scale DNS and validation
- Scientific AI workflows
:::
:::

## Highlights

- Co-architected **D-PARC** (Beerman, **Roy**, et al., 2026) &mdash; a physics-aware deformable-convolution neural operator; contributed the hybrid Lagrangian&ndash;Eulerian / deformable-convolution analogy.
- Leading the design of an **agentic microstructure-to-prediction sub-pipeline** as part of a closed-loop materials-discovery framework co-designed with my PI.
- 7 peer-reviewed journal papers across **Shock Waves**, **Journal of Applied Physics**, **Journal of Fluid Mechanics**, and **APL Machine Learning**.
- Production HPC campaigns resolving sub-micron damage features at **150M cells, 7,000 cores, ~3M CPU-hours at 15 nm grid resolution**.
- Mentoring **1 postdoc, 2 graduate students, and 1 undergraduate intern** at IIHR / University of Iowa; multi-institution research interfaces across AFOSR-MURI, ONR-MURI, UIUC, LANL, UVA, IIT Delhi, and IIT Madras.

## Education

**PhD, Mechanical Engineering** &mdash; University of Iowa, May 2025
Advisor: Prof. H.S. Udaykumar
*Dissertation: "A versatile framework for high-fidelity computations of high-speed reactive multi-material dynamics."*

**MS (Research), Aerospace Engineering** &mdash; Indian Institute of Technology Madras, June 2019
Advisors: Prof. Santanu Ghosh & Prof. G. Rajesh
*Thesis: "Investigation of Drag Reduction using a Porous Medium for a Normal-Shock / Boundary-Layer Interaction."*

**BTech, Mechanical Engineering** &mdash; National Institute of Technology Durgapur, May 2015

## Teaching

- **Teaching Assistant** (University of Iowa side) for *Physics-Aware Deep Learning &mdash; PINNs, neural operators, PARC, reduced-order models*, offered jointly with the University of Virginia School of Data Science (Spring 2026).

## Honors & Awards

- **Ballard & Seashore Dissertation Fellowship**, University of Iowa (2025)
- **1st Place (Graduate Category, Mechanical Engineering)**, Research Open House Poster Competition, College of Engineering, University of Iowa (2025)
- **Finalist (Top 13)**, University of Iowa Graduate College Three Minute Thesis (3MT) (2024)
- **Excellent Presentation Award**, 7th International Symposium on Energetic Materials and Their Applications, Tokyo, Japan (2021)
- **Dean's Graduate Fellowship**, College of Engineering, University of Iowa (2019)

## Adjacent capabilities

::: {.grid}
::: {.card}
### Adjacent SciML stack

Conversant with **PINNs**, **FNO**, **DeepONet**, differentiable simulation, active learning, and Bayesian optimization &mdash; built through architecture evaluation, collaboration with the UVA Baek group, and the joint PADL course.
:::

::: {.card}
### Scientific MLOps practice

Model registry, provenance and lineage tracking, RAG over scientific data, multi-agent orchestration for parallel research threads (literature triage, comparative-method research, build-script generation).
:::

::: {.card}
### LLM-augmented research workflow

GitHub Copilot CLI and VS Code agent mode (Claude, GPT) for code review and scheme prototyping; repo-scoped agents, version-controlled prompts and session transcripts, reproducible markdown&rarr;DOCX pipelines.
:::
:::
